LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: WITH EXCEPTION OF THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT WINE.COM, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FOR THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT WHETHER THE INCIDENTS ON WHICH THE HOSTILE WORK ENVIRONMENT CLAIM ARE BASED WERE SEVERE OR PERVASIVE OR BASED ON MORE THAN SPORADIC, ISOLATED EVENTS. FOR THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF RETALIATION BASED ON PROTECTED ACTIVITY. (SEE, E.G., AMF NO. 12, 36, 37.) FOR THE THIRD AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION, DEFENDANT PREDICATES ITS ARGUMENT ON THE ARGUMENTS RAISED IN THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. FOR THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT WHETHER DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN WAGE AND HOUR VIOLATIONS CONCERNING MEAL AND REST BREAKS AND UNCOMPENSATED OVERTIME. BOTH PARTIES HAVE VIOLATED THE RULES GOVERNING PAGE LENGTHS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT PLEADINGS. (CRC 3.1113(D).) THIS IS A SERIOUS IMPOSITION ON THE COURT. THESE ARE NOT "MINOR" RULES THAT THE PARTIES ARE FREE TO IGNORE WITH IMPUNITY, AND THEY CANNOT BE EVADED BY ADDING PAGES OF ARGUMENT TO OTHER REQUIRED DOCUMENTS. COUNSEL ARE DIRECTED TO READ THE RULES AND TO COMPLY WITH THEM ON ALL FUTURE FILINGS. FAILURE TO DO SO WILL CAUSE THE COURT TO WEIGH EXERCISING ITS DISCRETION NOT TO CONSIDER FUTURE OVERSIZED PLEADINGS. (CRC 3.1113(G) AND 3.1300(D).) THE COURT TAKES MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION ONLY. COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO SUBMIT 2 VERSIONS OF A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH COMPLIES WITH CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 437C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-22-2013 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: KYRA SUBBOTIN, ESQ., 510.923.0451, FOR PLAINTIFF CATHERINE WESTBROOKS.
ROBERT CARROL, ESQ., 415.984.8404, FOR DEFENDANT WINE.COM, INC.

RULING: MATTER ARGUED. AT CONCLUSION OF THE ARGUMENT, THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: WITH EXCEPTION OF THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT WINE.COM, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Denied. For the first cause of action, there are triable issues of fact whether the incidents on which the hostile work environment claim are based were severe or pervasive or based on more than sporadic, isolated events. For the second cause of action, Plaintiff has presented evidence of retaliation based on protected activity. (See, e.g., AMF no. 12, 36, 37.) For the third and fourth causes of action, Defendant predicates its argument on the arguments raised in the first and second causes of action. For the sixth cause of action, there are triable issues of fact whether Defendant engaged in wage and hour violations concerning meal and rest breaks and uncompensated overtime. Both parties have violated the rules governing page lengths for summary judgment pleadings. (CRC 3.1113(d).) This is a serious imposition on the Court. These are not "minor" rules that the parties are free to ignore with impunity, and they cannot be evaded by adding pages of argument to other required documents. Counsel are directed to read the rules and to comply with them on all future filings. Failure to do so will cause the Court to weigh exercising its discretion not to consider future oversized pleadings. (CRC 3.1113(g) and 3.1300(d).) THE COURT TAKES MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION ONLY. COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO SUBMIT 2 VERSIONS OF A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH COMPLIES WITH CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 437C(G). Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-27-2013 Continued To Oct-08-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
